calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-27-2023 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-15-2022 Continued To Mar-21-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-07-2022 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT SHAMBALA HEALING CENTER, INC.'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENED COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's demurrer to the amended complaint is sustained with leave to amend.

"The Unruh Act provides: All persons within the jurisdiction of this state ... no matter what their ... disability ... are entitled to the full and equal accommodations, advantages, facilities, privileges, or services in all business establishments of every kind whatsoever. (Civ. Code, SEC. 51, subd. (b).) A plaintiff can recover under the [Unruh Act] on two alternate theories: (1) a violation of the ADA (Civ. Code, SEC 51, subd. (f)); or (2) denial of access to a business establishment based on intentional discrimination." (Martinez v. Cot'n Wash, Inc. (2022) 81 Cal.App.5th 1026, 1035.) "Unless an Unruh Act claim is based on an ADA violation, the act requires a claimant to prove intentional discrimination. Thus, absent an ADA violation, the Unruh Act requires allegations supporting willful, affirmative misconduct with the specific intent to accomplish discrimination on the basis of [a protected trait]. Although evidence of disparate impact [may] be probative of intentional discrimination in some cases under the Unruh Act, it cannot alone establish such intent." (Id. at 1036.)

Here, plaintiff affirms in his opposition that he "does not have a cause of action for violation of the ADA." (Opp, 9:1-2.) Thus, under the Unruh Act, Plaintiff must show intentional discrimination. (See Martinez above.) In Martinez, the court held "we do not recognize a failure to address known discriminatory effects of a policy as alone sufficient to establish intentional discrimination under the Unruh Act." (Id. at 1038.) Plaintiff's allegations fail to rise to the level of Martinez (which were insufficient), where the defendant had knowledge of the website problems and failed to remedy them. (Id. at 1036.) Plaintiff alleges that he tried twice in 2021 to access the website, he encountered design faults that prevented him from using SRS, defendant failed to provide an accessible website, and as a result, he experienced difficulty, discomfort, or embarrassment. (FAC, 4-5.) The first amended complaint is devoid of facts that show willful, affirmative misconduct.
For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-22-2022 Continued To Dec-07-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:

Dan Weston, Esq. (415.788.4545; dvw@wykowskilaw.com) of Wykowski & Wood LLP for Defendant.

DEFENDANT SHAMBALA HEALING CENTER, INC'S. DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is continued to December 7, 2022 at 9:30am, Law and Motion Dept.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/RBU)
